entry.title: Re: SAP GRC Access Control 10.x/12.0 - Firefighter ID Controller Log Report Review Workflow
entry.content:
Hello Pau,

Thank you for this documentation, it helped me a lot.
Just for information, you have to remember to check that the WF-BATCH system user also has an email. The flow is not sent without this condition. I was stuck for a while...

Best regards

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hello,

If you want to download the Docker image, you get the following error:

System Windows 11 

PS C:\Users\thomas> docker pull sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022

What's next:
View a summary of image vulnerabilities and recommendations → docker scout quickview sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022
Error response from daemon: failed to resolve reference "docker.io/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022": docker.io/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022: not found
PS C:\Users\thomas> docker scout quickview sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022
i New version 1.14.0 available (installed version is 1.13.0) at https://github.com/docker/scout-cli
x Pull failed
level=error msg="Status: failed to get image sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022: failed to pull image sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRAIL_2022: GET https://index.docker.io/v2/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial/manifests/ABAPTRAIL_2022: MANIFEST_UNKNOWN: manifest unknown; unknown tag=ABAPTRAIL_2022, Code: 1"

what can I do to fix the problem?

Best Regards 

Thomas

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro
entry.content: The capabilities described in the tutorial are the primary way to do offline storage in SAP Build Apps. The integration to SAP Mobile Services offline capabilities will be the next step for this topic but it is not yet on the roadmap.

entry.title: Mask in CJ20N - "Screen" field
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help with an issue.

A few months ago I was working in a client that had the field "Screen" available in the project definition screen, where the mask of the project was visible (as per image below). The mask displayed in this field is the one defined in OPSJ.

Now I'm working in a different client and we would like to have this available as well, but the field is not visible in CJ20N. 

Does anyone knows how I can change this? Is there any customizing missing?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE - DSC Knowledge Bits - Cookbook for Advanced Shipping and Receiving
entry.content: Hi, it is possible but as described in chapter 2.2 the document only describes what is considered as best practise scenarios. Writing down every single variation would have blown the scope of this document too much.

entry.title: Re: [Blog Series] X.509 certificate-based authentication(mTLS) – Demystified
entry.content: Thanks for the writeup!

entry.title: API is used for logging into SuccessFactors- Can this be migrated to IAS as 3rd party idp?
entry.content:
Hello Experts-

My client uses a API- Grandid (Integrationsmetoder | Svensk e-identitet) for logging to SuccessFactors. As per a article on Common Super Domain - Migration to SAP Cloud Identity Authentication (IA... - SAP Community  SAP is asking all customers to migrate to IAS. What i want to know is can this Grandid be used as a Third party corporate IDP just like Azure and linked to IAS to login to SF?

If yes may i know the process how to perform this.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Component.getRouter() does not exist
entry.content:
use the generic UIComponent.getRouterFor method.

import UIComponent from "sap/ui/core/UIComponent";

onPress(): void {
        const router = UIComponent.getRouterFor(this);
        router.navTo("detail");
    }    
};

here earning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-activate-implementation-tools-and-methodology/describing-workstreams-in-cloud-implementation

we have

we have

entry.title: Re: Release of UI5 linter v1.0
entry.content: @PedroLazaro You can find the UI5 linter as part of SAP Fiori tools and thus also in the SAP Business Application Studio. See this blog post by @tashley for more details on this.

entry.title: Re: SAC App Designer - ExpandNode syntax help
entry.content: Hello, I cannot make this work either. Script that works with "Table_1.getDataSource().expandNode(....)" does not work with "Chart_1.getDataSource().expandNode(....)" if chart is waterfall chart with the same Datasource. Api Ref Guide to "expandNode" says "For Waterfall charts with one or more dimensions, the selection specifies one dimension and one measure. For Waterfall charts with one measure but no dimensions, the selection specifies this measure. For Waterfall charts with more than one measure but no dimensions, this operation is ignored." So it should work somehow with waterfall charts. If anybody managed to get this work or find out about the restrictions, I would be very greatful for sharing. Thanks

entry.title: India Regulatory rules compliance audit trail from DB setup
entry.content:
Dear Support,

We need clear informations and detailled technical guidance  regarding the implementation of the India regulatory rules related to the maintenance of an audit trail, as outlined in SAP note 3042258,

entry.title: Project Settlement with Parallel Ledgers (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
Reasons for Parallel Accounting

Companies often operate in diverse economic environments, and they might have various stakeholders, such as investors, regulators, and management, each requiring different types of financial information. To satisfy these differing needs, companies have to support different accounting principles in parallel. Here are some examples why:

  • Different countries have different financial reporting requirements and standards. 
    A multinational company operating in multiple jurisdictions might need to prepare financial statements that comply with each region's regulatory requirements.
  • Tax authorities may have their own set of accounting rules and principles distinct from financial accounting rules.
  • Internal management needs financial information tailored to specific operational or strategic goals.
    This might involve adjusting the standard accounting principles to reflect internal performance metrics or budgeting needs.
  • Supporting different accounting principles can enhance transparency and credibility with stakeholders.
    It can demonstrate that a company is diligent in maintaining accurate and reliable financial records.

In S/4HANA we update every posting according to the rules of the different parallel accounting principles in parallel ledgers. Each ledger can have a different accounting principle assigned.
There are different reasons why posted values can differ for different accounting principles. E.g.,

  • different capitalization rules for materials in the different GAAPs
  • different depreciation keys/useful life
  • ledger-specific postings, e.g. provisions

These different values also need to be reflected in follow-on processes like e.g.,

  • CO allocations like universal allocation, overhead calculation, or settlement
  • cost rate calculation
  • WIP and variance calculation

 

Differences in Settlement with and without Parallel Ledgers

But what exactly is the difference if settlement is enabled for parallel ledger handling?

The example in the picture above shows that, if different values are posted in the different ledgers, follow-on processes like for example settlement, have to take care of these different values and calculate the values to be settled separately per ledger. 

Without parallel ledger enablement, settlement is calculating the value to be settled only for the leading ledger, the settlement value from the leading ledger will then be posted in all parallel ledger. This leads to the following issues:

  • If different values have been posted for different ledgers, the settled project will only have a balance of zero in the leading ledger. 
  • The ledger specific values get lost for follow on processes.
    The picture above shows that without parallel accounting the value of the own produced fixed asset will be 175€ in all ledgers. With parallel accounting, the value for the fixed asset is different in the different ledgers.
    This example shows that even CO postings like project settlement influence balance sheet values and external reporting. This is why all postings need to support parallel ledgers.

In the public cloud edition, parallel ledgers and ledger-specific settlement is always active.

 

Ledger-Specific Settlement/ Distribution Rules

Defining ledger-specific settlement/ distribution rules is an optional functionality. For most of the use cases, ledger-independent rules will work fine (also with parallel accounting and ledger-specific settlement active).

A possible use case for ledger-specific rules is the settlement to fixed assets. The portion of costs that can be activated for the final asset, can differ in different GAAPs. In this case there is the need to define different rules for different ledgers.

If needed, the option for ledger-specific rules has to be activated in the configuration for the settlement profile:

Even if the option for ledger-specific rules is active, it is still possible to define ledger-independent rules.
A rule definition could look like this:

The system will first use ledger-specific rules, if no ledger-specific rules are defined for a ledger, the rule with ledger <blank> will be used.

 

Settlement History  

If a period is settled for the first time, the distribution rules that are valid for this period will be copied to the settlement history for the settled ledger and if they are relevant for distributing amounts to settlement receivers, they will be marked as used in the settlement period. For used rules the fields 'First Used' and 'Last Used' will be set accordingly.

If the same period is settled again, the settlement program will first check the settlement history and if distribution rules are already available for the settlement period and the settled ledger, those will be relevant for the settlement, even if the user has changed the settlement receivers in the meantime.

That means that changing the rule is not sufficient if a settlement already happened in the settlement period. If the new settlement receiver shall be taken into account for a settlement in the same period, the entry in the settlement history for the settlement period with the old receiver needs to first be removed by reversing all settlements that happened in the settlement period for the ledger. The last reversal will then either limit the validity of the distribution rule so that it is not valid for the settlement period or delete the entry in the settlement history completely, depending on whether the distribution rule had already been used in a previous period.

for further information see note 3066714

entry.title: Re: First Insights on UAE eInvoicing : What You Need to Know on the UAE DCTCE Model and the Rollout
entry.content:
Dear @Khalifa 

Nice informative blog I have one question related to it that What is SAP’s plan for the implementation of e-invoicing in the UAE? Will SAP adopt the Peppol network for compliance, or will it leverage SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) for integrating with the UAE's local regulatory requirements? Just like done in KSA.

entry.title: PDI BADI for Exclusion of Withholding Tax Items
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

does anybody know how this new feature works, its purpose and way of utilizing it?

The only explanation I found was this:

You can call Partner Development Infrastructure (PDI) - Business Add-Ins (BADI) in to SAP application studio to build reusable Add-Ins. Using PDI-BADI, you can now customize your logic and exclude the Invoices (Payment and Clearing documents) that are not required for Tax Reporting. The split invoice documents for Tax Reporting are sent back to SAP, and the withholding tax returns (856, 857,102) are posted based on the documents received.

Thanks

LM

entry.title: Re: Which products are addressed in the SAP Activate methodology for the Intelligent Enterprise road
entry.content:
Yes, I'm sure.
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-an-sap-btp-solution-architect/implementing-the-intelligent-enterprise


SAP's Offerings for Intelligent Enterprises

Summary

SAP offers a range of solutions that support companies in becoming an intelligent enterprise. These include:

  • SAP S/4HANA for real-time ERP.
  • SAP C/4HANA for customer experience.
  • SAP SuccessFactors for human resources.
  • SAP Ariba for procurement.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud for data analytics.

entry.title: Re: How can I print a check after issuing it using the 'P...
entry.content: Hi @ahsanali, the app doesn't support printing of check. You should use either Create Single Payment app or Manage Automatic Payments app

entry.title: Re: How to Restirct Only RM warehouse manual Goods Moveme...
entry.content: Dear Customer, If RM is a site, then you can go to work center Application and User Management view Business Users, select this user, click Edit - Access Rights, select tab Access Restrictions, find work center view Goods Movement in work center Internal Logistics, in the below select tab Detailed Restrictions, do not assign read and write access for the site RM. Henry SPA Technical Support

entry.title: SAP CAP: Wrong input for Date Type
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I recently had a requirement to update a Date type field in Hana DB Cloud table to a blank value from CAP UPDATE query.

Error:
Cannot set parameter at row: 1. Wrong input for DATE type

Query:

try {
   let tx = cds.tx();
   data = await tx.run(UPDATE(TABLE,
      {
         <key>
      }).with(
          {
              Date: '',
              <payload>
          }
      )).then(tx.commit);
} catch (err) {
}

Could anyone please let me know if this is possible to achieve in CAP?

I have alternate approach in mind but need to check on the above feasibility.

Thanks in advance,
Abhijeet

V2:

V4:

regards,

Lars

entry.title: Re: Allowing SNOTE implementations without modification rights in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Sales Order number not populated at the time of Adjus...
entry.content:
@VivekG 

When using late numbering BOs such as I_SALESORDERTP, you can get generated keys only at the point of save_modified method, so there is no way to set the key in the response body, as I understand it. However, keys are available in the response header 'sap-messages', which the consumers of your API can receive.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
@Th0mas review the tag

docker pull sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022

entry.title: Multiple elements in single SCORM file
entry.content:
Hello,

I am looking for confirmation or support with exporting SCORM files to be uploaded to Workday.

We have:

  • Practice and test
  • Multiple languages

If I put practice and test in a single scorm, and the item in Workday for this scorm is marked as mandatory, both practice and test must be completed for the learner to complete the Workday item.

Similarly, if I put 4 languages into a scorm and the item containing this scorm is mandatory, all languages must be completed for the learner to complete the Workday item.

In short, all elements in a single scorm must be completed to have the LMS item completed - is that true?

If not, how and where can I differentiate that in single scorm the test is mandatory and the practice optional?

entry.title: Re: Business Partner and Product Master Data Replication from On Premise to BTP using DRF
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question about your outbound configuration "PACK_SIZE_BULK". You use the value "100" for the package size. This means that 100 BPs are part of one message. But when there is an error with one of the business partners in this message, the other 99 BPs are also not processed, right?

entry.title: New Community Package: A Cloud Integration Alternative for SAP Process Orchestration Adapter Modules
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce a new Community Content integration package on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, specifically designed to streamline migrating from SAP Process Orchestration (PO) to SAP Cloud Integration.

This package brings scripts and integration flows to work as alternatives for the already known Adapter Modules that exist today on SAP Process Integration/Orchestration, such as transforming messages payloads from XML to CSV or Plain Text (e.g., fixed length, delimited) and vice-versa.

Currently, the package includes a collection of scripts that can be used as alternatives to the following SAP Process Orchestration Adapter Modules:

  • MessageTransformBean
  • XMLAnonymizer

Additionally, the package features sample Integration Flows for various use cases, along with detailed configuration guides for each script. These resources will help you understand how to effectively integrate the scripts into your scenarios.

In summary, the approach involves adding a Content Modifier before the Groovy Script step to set up properties used as configuration inputs for the script. The property names are mostly aligned with those in SAP Process Orchestration, making the transition easier for those familiar with the existing modules.

Looking ahead, we plan to extend this package with additional Groovy scripts and integration flow alternatives for other modules, such as:

  • IDOCFlatToXmlConvertor
  • IDOCXmlToFlatConvertor
  • AddSOAPHeaderBean
  • PayloadSwapBean

Whether you are migrating existing SAP Process Integration/Orchestration scenarios or building new ones directly on Cloud Integration, this package offers valuable support and enables you to take advantage of modernized approaches to transformation.

Access it now on Business Accelerator Hub and feel free to share feedback: Overview | Message Transformation Utilities | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 

Please note that a community package is provided as a standalone component under the Apache License, v. 2.0 and, is not part of any SAP product. SAP does not support Community Content. Please raise any issues at the associated GitHub project.

You can find the official release plans for SAP product development on SAP Roadmap Explorer. Here you can find features of this package and when they will be released as a standard feature in the SAP Integration Suite.

If one of these features is required in the SAP Integration Suite in a timely manner but not listed in the SAP Roadmap Explore, we therefore ask you to create a “Feature Improvement” on SAP Customer Influence – Campaign: SAP Integration Suite or vote for it, if exactly same “Feature Improvement” is already available. All “Feature Improvements” and especially their votes have a significant influence on the prioritization of the features to be delivered in the SAP Integration Suite.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP RAP managed batch service is failing due to d...

@gchavala 

Why not use UUID as a key field?

entry.title: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exam
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I've recorded a demo on how to create your SAP Universal ID, "purchase" a SAP Certification exam attempt using a campaign code and schedule your SAP Certification exam in one flow.

Some tips:

  • Be sure to select your country before attempting to add any item to your shopping basket on training.sap.com 
  • Have a secondary email address ready to connect with your SAP Universal ID
  • Consult our troubleshooting guide if you have any issues with the campaign code process
  • Contact sapcertification@questionmark.com if there are problems with your exam scheduling or experience

Watch the demo:

Timestamps:

0:00 - Create your SAP Universal ID (UID)
1:11 - Use campaign code to book a free SAP Certification Exam attempt
3:22 - Link additional email to your UID
5:48 - Schedule your exam
10:44 - Cancel your appointment

Here is the single SAP Certification attempt product to check out with. This attempt applies to all SAP exams. There is no need to look for a specific exam title until later in the process.

Best of luck with your exam!

-David

PS - if you do not pass your exam, take a screenshot of your scorecard before closing it out. It will help you understand which areas you need to re-study.

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content: I am looking for the meaning of life or the reason for changing the name of SAP products... I don't know what is easier to understand 🤔

entry.title: Re: Fiori Currency Conversion Issue
entry.content:
Have you created a custom currency code type definition in table TCURC ?  E.g. using the configuration menu in transaction SPRO. 

Manipulating decimal places on the front-end only in this way is very risky. When you change the decimal places you change the meaning of the value. That affects everywhere that value is used, e.g. when converting from local to foreign currency. 

Retail customers often use 4 decimal place currencies like this - and for that you need a custom currency code type definition. Then all the currency conversions will work reliably system wide. 

From what I understand of your approach this is really ABAP fundamentals and actually nothing to do with SAP Fiori - SAP Fiori is applying the conversion correctly (its actually just reflecting what the ABAP currency conversion is automatically doing via the CDS View) . You might want to ask your question using the ABAP tag.

entry.title: Time difference issue crystal reports
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please assist as I am still new to Crystal.

 

I have a clocking report , that I need to calculate the difference between times.

See below.

I need to calculate the difference between the out time and the in time.

I use "dateadd("n",{Clockings.CLO_TIME}/100/60,today())" for my time formula.

 

Please assist.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI in Eclipse on Mac cannot display HTML or acce...

entry.title: Re: open attachment from webclient
entry.content:
Hi  SuHsiang

Attachments are supported in web client starting from SAP Business One version 2208.

Please upgrade to 2208 or latest to use the attachment in web client.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Re: AES Encryption in ABAP
entry.content:
Hello @monalisa_biswal 

 

Thank you for this blog post, it was very helpful. However, we have a business requirement where the need to do this encoding WITHOUT the IV. Any pointers on this will be helpful.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud. How to deliver my collection to customer?
entry.content:
Hi.
Customer can create some CDS view in Fiori without me, but it will be view with OData2 service. As I learned, I cannot create OData4 service in Fiori.
I must use ADT(Eclipse) or BTP Cockpit->"SAP Business Application Studio". Is it full list of tools for OData4 service creating?
Also I must develop my view and service in DEV tenant. Then I can put my view and sevice into collection and push it to my TEST tenant.
Then to my PROD tenant.
Doc: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/aa60b129af7b4ce8ae052618c8315d29.html

The question is what mechanism should I use if I want to deliver my collection to customers? Not to my own production tenant.

Without device id, old mobile app could not be used.

Nidhi Singh

entry.title: AES Encryption - without IV
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to encrypt a text with a secret key - 'without passing the IV'. Referring to https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/aes-encryption-in-abap/ba-p/13401465

we were able to arrive at the logic along with the IV, however without the IV the correct encoding doesnt take place.  

Any pointers on how we can achieve this will be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Last Logon Time
entry.content: Yes, you would need to create a DB user for each user. For now there is no statistics like in BW.

entry.title: Database explorer, failed generating SQL based on prompt
entry.content:
Hello,
I'm trying to use the new genAI feature in SAP Build Code Database Explorer to generate SQL based on prompt.

What ever the prompt, I'm always getting the following error: "Unable to generate the SQL statement. Error from LLM: Failed to get embeddings. Try rephrasing the prompt."

entry.title: Re: Festkomma-Arithmetik in ABAP unter SAP S/4HANA: Was Sie wissen sollten
entry.content:
This blog post is full of errors, imprecisions and confusions (see below), especially the ABAP program attribute "fixed-point arithmetic" isn't related to decimal imprecision (confusion with binary floating-point numbers, i.e. variables of type F), it's about choosing between fixed-point numbers and "no-decimal numbers" (that would be another blog post to explain the real meaning of the program attribute "fixed-point arithmetic").

I suggest retiring this blog post to not mislead ABAP developers. Or instead, you could just say "don't use the data type F for accounting calculations".

Thank you.

  • "In ABAP, this is especially important to avoid precision errors that could occur when performing numeric operations with floating-point numbers"
    • Here you are talking about "binary floating-point numbers", to not confuse with ABAP 7.02 "decimal floating-point numbers" which don't have these "decimal" precision errors. But what is the relationship with the program attribute "fixed-point arithmetic"?
  • "Fixed-point arithmetic can be enabled or disabled in an ABAP program"
    • It's enabled by default for new programs since ABAP 7.40, you cannot disable it. Only very old and obsolete programs have it disabled.
  • "when working with numeric fields such as P (Packed Number) or DEC (Decimal)"
    • P is the only ABAP type concerned. The DDIC data types DEC, QUAN and CURR correspond to P.
  • "Under SAP S/4HANA [...] there are some optimizations and performance improvements, particularly in relation to the HANA database."
    • There is no relationship between the ABAP program attribute "fixed point arithmetic" and HANA, and so there are no implied optimizations and performance improvements.
  • "4. Practical Benefits of Fixed-Point Arithmetic"
    • You are only talking about binary floating-point numbers versus packed numbers/decimal floating-point numbers. The ABAP program attribute Fixed-Point Arithmetic doesn't lead to decimal imprecisions, all ABAP programs are accurate to the cent hopefully.
  • "5. Specific Considerations in SAP S/4HANA"
    • The program attribute "fixed-point arithmetic" doesn't prevent you from using binary floating-point numbers.
  • "6. When Should Fixed-Point Arithmetic Not Be Used?"
    • Again, you mean not using the ABAP type F, it doesn't concern the program attribute "fixed-point arithmetic".

entry.title: Re: Where can i have SAP SuccessFactors Incentive managem...
entry.content:

Unfortunately we do not provide SAP Learning System Accesss for SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management.

Margit

entry.title: Re: Create infoset based on indirect join
entry.content:
Take IFLOT-OBJNR with IFLOT-TPLNR = IFLOS-TPLNR and IFLOT-OBJNR = VIBDOBJASS-OBJNRTRG

hth

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for MacOS
entry.content:
Hello Stevica,
Have you installed SAP Secure Login Client for mac OS as well?

entry.title: Strategic Decision Paths for Managing Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculations (SCT, SFM, EHS-EM)
entry.content:
Summary

This article guides businesses through the architectural decision-making process regarding which SAP sustainability solutions to use for calculating corporate carbon footprints according to the Greenhouse Gas Protocol. It focuses on three SAP Sustainability solutions: SAP Sustainability Control Tower, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP EHS Environment Management. The aim of the article is to clarify the best solution paths based on different customer business requirements within the broader scope of carbon footprint calculations.  

 

Business Requirements

A corporate carbon footprint is the total amount of GHG emissions directly or indirectly caused by a company’s activities. Emissions are usually calculated for a specific period, such as a calendar year.  

Regulatory frameworks like CSRD, ESRS, EFRS, SEC are evolving quickly and demanding corporate level reporting. Organizations are required to communicate and demonstrate accountability for their impacts on the environment, economy and people and to create a transparent data foundation for decarbonization of own operations and supply chain. This is done by calculating the Greenhouse Gases, which are commonly structured along scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions according to the GHG-Protocol. 

 

Solution Options

SAP offers three primary solution paths for calculating corporate carbon footprint (CCF): 

  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Ideal for low maturity customers, it offers a single solution for entry level CCF calculations based on manual data collection as well as ESG reporting capabilities. With the combination of CCF calculation and reporting, customers are well set for complying with respective regulation.  
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Suitable for all customers, and ideal for more advanced customers, enabling them to perform highly mature and granular corporate carbon footprint (CCF) as well as product carbon footprint (PCF) calculations  
  • SAP EHS-Environment Management: Ideal for customers from specific, especially energy intensive industries such as steel or cement that have to manage a Greenhouse Gas Emissions inventory at facility level to comply with regulations such as US EPA Mandatory Reporting Rule, EU-ETS requirements or government issued environmental permits or licenses to operate. 

 

Characterization of the Solution Options

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT)  

Focus: Enables organizations to manage their ESG (Environment, Social and Government) metrics including ESG data acquisition, monitoring and steering ESG targets and reporting on sustainability performance  

CCF Focus: Calculate scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions for an entry-level CCF based on manual data collection via SAP’s guided forms, uploaded into SCT 

Outcome:   

  • Report a CCF as needed for many regulations, like CSRD  
  • Embed CO2e into strategic decision-making by using KPI reporting based on established frameworks and emerging reporting standards  
  • Set, monitor and steer towards sustainability targets  
  • Provide business units dedicated insights on their sustainability performance and the ability to identify improvement areas  

 SAP Sustainability Footprint Management (SFM)  

Focus: Provide granular transparency of an organization’s carbon footprint (CO2e) on product, value chain and corporate level for mandatory or voluntary requirements. Move away from using averages to using actual primary data for footprint calculation.  

CCF Focus: Advanced capabilities of calculating scope 1,2 and 3 emissions via SAP’s guided CCF forms or/and leveraging transactional data right out of the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system or actual supplier data shared via SAP Sustainability Data Exchange for more automation and granularity. Includes an advanced transport module and AI capabilities for Emission Factor mapping. Can also be used to calculate a simple CCF first, then increase granularity over time.  

Outcome:  

  • Calculate scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions with advanced methodologies and ERP centric data collection to improve process efficiency and accuracy of results.  
  • Reuse transactional data from SAP S/4HANA to automate and scale calculation.  
  • Use supplier specific data for advanced accuracy via SDX integration.  
  • Establish a transparent and auditable process for calculating CO2e from data collection to reporting.  

 SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EHS Environment Management  

Focus: Manage all GHG emissions inventory to fulfill legal regulatory and permit compliance as well as voluntary and corporate requirements.  

CCF Focus: Scope 1 and 2 emissions based on actuals. Selected scope 3 emissions can be calculated with a consulting-based approach.  

Outcome:  

  • Used for calculating actual greenhouse gas emissions (for all individual greenhouse gases, e.g. CO2, MH4, N2O, HFCs, NMVOCs, PFCs, SF6, CO2e) and preparing inventories 
  • Equations and emissions factors specified by regulatory agencies are available as example content (e.g., US, Australia, EU). 
  • Emissions can be estimated using a variety of methods (see table below) including integration ability with continuous emission monitoring systems. 
  • Calculate and report on environmental data beyond emissions, such as water, wastewater, or waste management. 
  • Enables to maintain operational continuity by supporting permits and regulatory compliance. 
  • Establish transparency and audibility into your greenhouse gas emissions management process (data collection, calculations to reporting) 
  • Monitor emissions exceedances to thresholds and record deviations for further corrective actions and investigations. 

 

Criteria to determine the most suitable target solution

  • Is the client on or planning to move to S/4HANA Cloud with RISE or GROW?  
  • Is the client able to implement public cloud solutions for carbon footprint calculations?  
  • What is the maturity level and appetite of the client in the context of CCF?  
  • Which GHG protocol scope categories are in focus/most important for the client?  
  • What are GHG levels of breakdown?  
  • What are the client’s current and future sources of data for Scope 1,2,3 emission calculations?  
  • What is the client’s business - manufacturer vs service provider?  
  • Based on the client’s business model and operations, is there value in calculating not only corporate but also product carbon footprints?  
  • Does the client currently run SAP EHS for any process supporting sustainability?  

 

Evaluation Spreadsheet

 

Criteria/Options  

SCT  

SFM  

EHS-EM  

RISE or GROW prerequisite (customer needs to have a RISE or GROW license in order to purchase the solution)  

Yes  

Yes  

No  

CCF Maturity Level  

Low 

Low, medium and high  

High (for scopes 1 and 2)  

Scope of carbon management  

CCF (CO2e) data collection, calculation, analytics, reporting; target setting and monitoring  

  

Integrated CCF and PCF (CO2e) data collection, calculation, analytics  

  

Data collection, calculation, analytics and reporting of environmental aspects including and beyond CO2e, such as air pollutants, water pollutants, GHG emission, and waste.  

  • GHG emissions inventory management
  • Air emissions management 
  • Waste management  
  • Water & wastewater management  

GHG Levels of Breakdown  

Only Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e)  

  

Only Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e)  

  

Individual GHG emissions inventory (e.g. CO2, MH4, N2O, HFCs, NMVOCs, PFCs, SF6)   

 

Able to convert all GHG emission amounts into Carbon Dioxide equivalent (CO2e) values  

Focus-Industry  

Professional services or GROW customers 

 

 

Consumer Products, Retail, Automotive, Industrial Manufacturing, Mill and Mining, Chemicals 

Oil&Gas, Chemicals, Mill and Mining, Utilities, Pharmaceuticals, Consumer Products, (ENR industries) 

Data sources for Scope 1,2,3 emissions?  

Manual data entries based on simple forms 

 Manual data entries based on specialized forms,  

ERP transactional and master data (from SAP S/4HANA),  

Supplier specific data ( via SDX) 

Public APIs File import 

ERP Data/Supplier Specific Data / Operational Data (Meter level data e.g. Natural Gas usage) via integration with Data Historians and Operation Control Systems 

Calculation methods 

Spend based and  

average data 

Spend based  

Average data based 

Supplier specific 

Hybrid 

For more details on scope 1 and 2 emissions à Calculation Methods by GHG Category | SAP Help Portal 

Average data 

Emissions from fuel combustion 

Tier 1 methodology 

Process emissions 

CEMS (continuous emission monitoring systems) 

Mass balance approach 

Site-specific emission factors 

 

Calculating carbon footprint at product level?  

 No 

Yes  

No  

 

Decision Tree

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, selecting the appropriate SAP solution for corporate carbon footprint calculations depends on your organization's specific requirements and where you are on your sustainability journey. SAP Sustainability solutions can be used flexibly and in a modular approach to provide you with the capabilities needed at every point of your sustainability journey.    

 Recommendations Based on Customer Focus/Scope  

  • Full, entry-level corporate carbon footprint calculation and sustainability reporting in one single solution: SAP SCT is the solution recommended.  
  • Full, in-depth corporate as well as product carbon footprint calculations: SAP SFM is the solution recommended if customer needs or wants to measure their corporate and also product carbon footprints on a granular level in an ERP centric and scalable approach.  
  • All environmental aspects including GHG emissions and beyond with air pollutants, water pollutants and waste: EHS – EM is the solution recommended if customer needs or wants to manage and monitor each individual GHG emissions inventory down to equipment level, for example for regulatory permit or operational license reasons. 

  
Please note that these are complementary and integrated solutions. Each solution can be used standalone for its most prominent use cases, or in combination for additional, complementary use cases. Due to their integration, solution data can be leveraged when used together: for example, a customer who uses EHS-EM for regulatory license/ permit reasons, can also re-use their EHS-EM scope 1 and 2 data for calculating their CCF in SFM. 

If interested, follow our Senior Product Manager for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, Nico Wottke, for quarterly blog post updates on SFM: About NicoWottke - SAP Community

entry.title: detail data on Custom Code Analytics on SAP for Me
entry.content:
We are using PCE S/4 Hana and have no soultion manager on-premise, I can find some aggregated custom code data on   https://me.sap.com/app/customcodeanalytics,

For example, There is a  dimension "Custom Code Usage by Object Type" which calculate used and unused programs, I want to know which custom codes are not using since maybe one year ago, how to show the detail information?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: detail data on Custom Code Analytics on SAP for Me
entry.content: For more details on https://me.sap.com/app/customcodeanalytics, please click button 'Service Request' on top right corner to schedule a service.

entry.title: Re: Versions in Header tab
entry.content: Did you solve the problem at last?

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP’s Business and Solution transformation methodologies?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Business and Solution Transformation Tools and Methodology learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the SAP’s solution and business transformation methodologies, its associated solutions and offerings and the interplay between those offerings.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to:

  • Explain the need and hurdles of Business transformation within an enterprise.
  • Understand SAP’s Business and Solution transformation methodologies and the associated solutions and offerings.
  • Learn the benefits of SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio, SAP Lean IX and Tricentis and its integration

Prerequisites

  • Managing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions
  • Analyzing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions
  • Learning Journey: Learning to Use the LeanIX Tool for Successful Enterprise Architecture

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Custom field in service entry sheet Fiori App
entry.content: Its on premise system.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: Same here. Missing badges for #201926 and Petoberfest. Please have a look. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Currency Conversion Issue
entry.content:
I think I am unable to clearly state my requirement. For JPY, I want to show correct value, using annotations. How to do it?  guess i'm using wrong annotation.

Value Stored JPY : 15494732.37 

Value Shown : 1549473.237

Value should be shown : 1549473237 ( if without decimal val currency).

Yes TCURC/TCURX has custom values for custom currencies. 

And again, thanks for your reply and link to blog 🙂

entry.title: Times recordings are always sent to project manager for approval, not to the Task Responsible
entry.content:
An employee records time on a project task and releases them. You find that these times recordings are always sent to project manager for approval, not to the Person Responsible at task level.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Times recordings are always sent to project manager f...
entry.content:
All the time and expenses incurred on the project is always needed to be approved by the project manager, not by the Task person responsible.

Irrespective of the person responsible on the task level, all the time recordings will be sent to the project manager (who is responsible for the entire project).

Hence all the project time recordings are always sent to project manager for approval.

Please refer KBA: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2666757

entry.title: SAP error
entry.content:
Error Message:

Outbound Delivery not created!
An item with no delivery quantity is not permitted. Item will be deleted.
No instance of object type OutboundDelivery has been created. External re


The sale only generated the Purchasing Doc. but fails to generate the delivery.
Any advice on how to proceed?

- stock checked through MMBE that is also available in unrestricted us

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP not allowing to delete DN
entry.content: SAP not allowing to delete DN that did not ship, but SAP is showing packaging unit

entry.title: Re: Request ID in Replication Flows
entry.content: Hi Tobias, so you have a scheduled replication flow which runs every hour? Then schedule it via Task Chain. If you have the replication flow set for permanent / real-time then you will need some DB trigger based function, else you can use the data flow, but with my described way to fetch the 'request ID' from a custom table. Br, Martin

entry.title: Dimension Hierarchy Dropdown total
entry.content: I have a dimension ( Called "Fonte de financiamento", whitch has 3 "children" "RP", "RG", and "OE", than it has the other children of this 3 things) in a row on DropDown level 1 showing all totals ( I selected on Show/Hide of the dimesion ), but when i select level 2 dropdown, the totals dissapears. When i use drop down on one of those 3, it continues to appear, but when i select 2, the totals do the same and dissapear. What is the problem, and how can i fix it?

entry.title: Re: WBS - Project with Revenue - Margin Analysis not updated
entry.content: Hi Owen/SAP Team, could you please help to resolve issue, based on the screenshots provided?

entry.title: Re: Making an OnPremise ABAP CI/CD with Github Actions
entry.content:
Yes, TRM focus is delivery of add-ons.

This demo has the propagation to what in a real life environment would be customer systems, however this step can also be skipped depending on what you're working with.

If you got any questions regarding TRM feel free to reach out to me!

entry.title: Re: Returning Excel with a CAP service
entry.content: I have tried creating the stream the same way, as in lines 14f, but it get the same issue that the data from the stream is not actually read, but returned as 'value: "[object Readable]"'. For reference, I am using CDS version 8.3.0

entry.title: Where can I learn how to move from an existing Data Warehouse landscape to SAP Datasphere?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Modernizing Your Data Warehouse Landscape – From SAP BW to SAP Datasphere learning journey.

Overview
In this Learning Journey, you will learn which scenarios exist to leverage current investment and modernize your data warehouse landscape. Your main options are the greenfield approach, the hybrid approach with SAP BW/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA, and the migration and conversion approach to SAP BW bridge. You will learn about use cases, value propositions, and steps for these approaches. The learning journey contains step-by-step procedures and videos explaining how you implement the connections and objects in a real system for each scenario.

Learning objectives
After completing this Learning Journey, you will be able to understand different paths and technological options to move from an existing Data Warehouse landscape based on SAP NetWeaver BW or SAP BW/4HANA to SAP Datasphere and SAP BW bridge.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring SAP Datasphere
  • Recommended:
    • Modeling in SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge
    • BW410
 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Is there a report that defines the responsibilities of SAP in ECP?

Hi @praveen7304 
An agent is currently processing your ticket and is analyzing why the access code has not yet been sent to you for redemption.
Kind regards
Margit

I would imagine the IDOC to Flat would be an iflow with process direct that could cache the Meta data. 

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I am a Mechanical Engineer career shifter from a Manufacturing Team Leader to Business Architecture Analyst. I am willing to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification. I will start learning more on SAP PP/QM Training modules.

Thank you

Efrenel

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_jung ,

Any issue with points update in gameboard? Points are not getting updated.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Currency Conversion Issue
entry.content:
Ok so I see your issue now. Thank you for the clarification!

Recommend you first double-check your custom currency code is working correctly by running report RCURTEST in GUI to check. See SAP Note 2561100 - Currency Conversion in CDS Queries - Could not determine valid conversion rate for value(s) ...

Assuming this is ok... then as explained in the ABAP CDS keyword documentation for amounts https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_758_index_htm/7.58/en-US/index.htm?file=abencds_amount_field.htm yes you do need to apply the mandatory  annotation @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : true to the currency code field

I'm not sure why you think it's unusable though? It's just commented out... You should un-comment the line and reactivate the CDS View. 

That should solve it. Remember you can have multiple annotations before a field.

entry.title: Help with our Project
entry.content:
We were given a project more of a group/individual project and I have to score 72 to pass this training. I already have a code but there are a lot of errors. I'll be attaching my code here including the requirements for the program. I hope you guys can help me with my program. We will be presenting this Oct 7, 2024 so I hope you guys can help me before our presentation day. 

REPORT TEST_PROGRAM.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_output,
         matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
         maktx TYPE makt-maktx,
         werks TYPE marc-werks,
         name1 TYPE t001w-name1,
         ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
         ernam TYPE mara-ernam,
         laeda TYPE mara-laeda,
         aenam TYPE mara-aenam,
         pstat TYPE marc-pstat,
         dismm TYPE marc-dismm,
         dispo TYPE marc-dispo,
         disls TYPE marc-disls,
         beskz TYPE marc-beskz,
         herkl TYPE marc-herkl,
         losgr TYPE marc-losgr,
         perkz TYPE marc-perkz,
         mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
         mbrsh TYPE mara-mbrsh,
         matkl TYPE mara-matkl,
         bismt TYPE mara-bismt,
         meins TYPE mara-meins,
       END OF ty_output.

DATA: lt_output TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_output,
      ls_output TYPE ty_output,
      lt_fieldcat TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
      ls_fieldcat TYPE lvc_s_fcat.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE text-001.
  PARAMETERS: p_matno TYPE mara-matnr OBLIGATORY,
             p_ers TYPE mara-ersda OBLIGATORY.
  PARAMETERS: p_plant TYPE marc-werks OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

PARAMETERS: p_write RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1,
            p_alv RADIOBUTTON GROUP rb1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_desc AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT ' '.

CLASS lhc_material DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS:
      validate_material IMPORTING iv_material TYPE matnr,
      validate_created_on IMPORTING iv_created_on TYPE ersda,
      validate_plant IMPORTING iv_plant TYPE MARC-WERKS,
      retrieve_data IMPORTING iv_material TYPE matnr
                     RETURNING VALUE(et_final_output) TYPE TABLE OF ty_output.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_material IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD validate_material.
    IF iv_material IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT SINGLE matnr FROM mara
        INTO @DATA(lv_matnr)
        WHERE matnr = @iv_material.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE 'Invalid Material' TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD validate_created_on.
    IF iv_created_on IS NOT INITIAL AND iv_created_on > sy-datum.
      MESSAGE 'Date specified is in the future, enter a valid date' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD validate_plant.
    IF iv_plant IS NOT INITIAL.
      SELECT SINGLE werks FROM t001w
        INTO @DATA(lv_werks)
        WHERE werks = @iv_plant.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE 'Invalid Plant' TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD retrieve_data.
    DATA: lt_makt TYPE TABLE OF makt,
          ls_makt TYPE makt,
          lt_t001w TYPE TABLE OF t001w,
          ls_t001w TYPE t001w.

    SELECT a~matnr, a~ersda, a~ernam, a~laeda, a~aenam, a~mtart, a~mbrsh, a~matkl, a~bismt, a~meins,
           c~werks, c~pstat, c~dismm, c~dispo , c~disls, c~beskz, c~herkl, c~losgr, c~perkz
      FROM mara AS a
      INNER JOIN marc AS c
      ON a~matnr = c~matnr
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE et_final_output
      WHERE a~matnr = iv_material.

    SELECT maktx FROM makt
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_makt
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN et_final_output
      WHERE matnr = et_final_output-matnr AND spras = sy-langu.

    SELECT name1 FROM t001w
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE lt_t001w
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN et_final_output
      WHERE werks = et_final_output-werks AND spras = sy-langu.

    LOOP AT et_final_output INTO DATA(ls_output).
      READ TABLE lt_makt INTO ls_makt WITH KEY matnr = ls_output-matnr.
      IF sy -subrc =  0.
        ls_output-maktx = ls_makt-maktx.
      ENDIF.

      READ TABLE lt_t001w INTO ls_t001w WITH KEY werks = ls_output-werks.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        ls_output-name1 = ls_t001w-name1.
      ENDIF.

      MODIFY et_final_output INDEX sy-tabix FROM ls_output.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  DATA: lv_matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
        lv_ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
        lv_werks TYPE marc-werks.

  lv_matnr = p_matno.
  lv_ersda = p_ers.
  lv_werks = p_plant.

  CALL METHOD lhc_material=>validate_material
    EXPORTING
      iv_material = lv_matnr.

  CALL METHOD lhc_material=>validate_created_on
    EXPORTING
      iv_created_on = lv_ersda.

  CALL METHOD lhc_material=>validate_plant
    EXPORTING
      iv_plant = lv_werks.

  IF p_alv = 'X'.
    DATA(lt_output) = lhc_material=>retrieve_data( iv_material = lv_matnr ).

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MATNR'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MAKTX'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'WERKS'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_field cat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'NAME1'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'ERSDA'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'ERNAM'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'LAEDA'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'AENAM'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'PSTAT'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'DISMM'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'DISPO'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'DISLS'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls _fieldcat-fieldname = 'BESKZ'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'HERKL'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'LOSGR'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'PERKZ'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MTART'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_field cat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MBRSH'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MATKL'.
    ls_field cat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'BISMT'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MEINS'.
    ls_fieldcat-tabname   = 'LT_OUTPUT'.
    ls_fieldcat-key       = 'X'.
    APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

    TRY.
        CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
          EXPORTING
            it_outtab                     = lt_output
            it_fieldcat                   = lt_fieldcat
          EXCEPTIONS
            program_error                 = 1
            OTHERS                        = 2.
      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
        MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDIF.

 

As for the requirements: 

1.     In SE38, create a program.

  • Use naming convention, Y<SAP ID>_MMR_MATREP, where SAP ID = User ID, MM = Material Management, R = Report (Ex: Y19999999_MMR_MATREP)
  • Set program attributes:
    1.      Title: ‘Material Master Report - Plant View’
    2.      Type: ‘Executable Program’.
  •      Package: $tmp or save as local object

2.     Create the screen criteria.

  • Create two select-options: Material (MARA-MATNR) and Created On (MARA-ERSDA).
  • Create one parameter: Plant (MARC-WERKS).
  • Use the table field’s data element short text as screen labels. Do not use technical names as labels.
  • Use SELECTION-SCREEN BLOCK (open the link to read further) syntax to enclose the input fields and checkbox. Add a frame and title. Use text symbol TEXT-T01 as title. Fill TEXT-T01 with ‘Criteria:’.
  • Add two radio buttons: ‘Display report with WRITE’ and ‘Display report as ALV’. Select by default the radio button with the ALV display.
  • Add a checkbox called ‘Display Material Description’ and default it to false.
  • Enclose the radio buttons and checkbox in another selection-screen block. Use text symbol TEXT-T02 as title. Fill TEXT-T01 with ‘Select a View:’.

 

Sample output:

3.     Create a local model class. Define STATIC method for each validation below in PUBLIC SECTION. Use method parameter to import the select-option or parameter into each method.

  1. Validate Material input.
    1.      When a value is entered in the screen, check if value exists in MARA. If no value exists, display error message ‘Invalid Material’ on the status bar. The selection screen must still be visible and editable.
    2.      When no value entered in the screen, no validation needed.
  2. Validate Created On input.
    1.      When a value entered in the screen is greater than the current date (sy-datum), display error message ‘Date specified is in the future, enter a valid date’.
    2.      When no value entered in the screen, no validation needed.
  3. Validate Plant input.
    1.      When a value is entered in the screen, check if value exist in T001W. If no value exists, display error message ‘Invalid Plant’ on the status bar. The selection screen must still be visible and editable.
    2.      When no value entered in the screen, no validation needed.

 

Sample syntax for error message (in event block AT SELECTION-SCREEN).

  • MESSAGE TEXT-XXX TYPE 'E'. (open the link to read further)

 

Hint:

  • Use the appropriate SELECT statement to validate if a value exists in a database table. Use SELECT SINGLE or SELECT UP TO 1 ROWS ENDSELECT and use WHERE to compare the correct field with the input.
  • To transfer select-option values into a method, use data dictionary table type rseloption as data type for method parameters. Make sure to apply same data type to the select-option before transferring into the method.

4.     After SELECTION-SCREEN blocks, add event block AT SELECTION-SCREEN (open the link to read further). In AT SELECTION-SCREEN, call the STATIC methods created earlier to validate screen inputs. Do unit testing and make sure validation are working well.

5.     In model class, create INSTANCE method to retrieve and process data from database.

  1. Create an internal table with data dictionary type RSELOPTION. Use the ABAP 740 syntax, append one entry where field SIGN = ‘I’, OPTION ‘EQ’, LOW = screen criteria plant.

 

  1. Get data from table MARC and MARA using JOIN (see ‘Report Fields’ below to check what relevant fields to retrieve). . Check what similar fields (primary key = foreign key) in these two tables to use for the JOIN. Filter with screen criteria material and created on whether user entered values or not. Filter with internal table from 5.a that stores the screen criteria material type whether user entered values or not. Filter with internal table from 5.a that stores the screen criteria material type whether user entered values or not. If there is no data returned, issue error message ‘No data found.’

 

Sample syntax for error message (in event block START-OF-SELECTION)

MESSAGE TEXT-XXX TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE ‘E’ (open the link to read further)

LEAVE LIST-PROCESSING (open the link to read further)

 

  1. Check if internal table from previous step has entries before proceeding to next steps.

 

  1. Get MATNR, MAKTX from MAKT using FOR ALL ENTRIES in internal table retrieved from 5.b. Check what similar fields in these two tables to use for the FOR ALL ENTRIES. Filter field SPRAS with SY-LANGU. Default system language is ‘EN’ for English. Sort the internal table based on primary keys.

 

  1. Get plant and plant name from T001W using FOR ALL ENTRIES in internal table retrieved from 5.b. Filter SPRAS with SY-LANGU. Default system language is ‘EN’ for English. Sort the internal table based on primary keys.

 

  1. Use LOOP and READ to transfer all field values retrieved from multiple database tables from previous steps into a final internal table. In Plant field/column, concatenate the Plant and Plant Name separated with dash.

 

  1. Export the final internal table.

 

Hint: You may declare internal tables in class definition so that it can be accessible to every method inside a class.

6.     After AT SELECTION-SCREEN, add event block START-OF-SELECTION. In START-OF-SELECTION(open the link to read further), create an object or instance of the model class. Call the method that retrieve data. Do unit testing and make sure logic is working well.

7.     Create a local view class. In view class, create INSTANCE method to display report fields using WRITE and import the final internal table from previous method. And create another INSTANCE METHOD to display reports using SALV(open the link to read further) and import the final internal table from previous method as well. Check below for report fields to be displayed.

  1. Follow column label/description below.
  2. Follow column sequence below on how they will be displayed.
  3. For WRITE method, Write the column headers/descriptions first before writing the final internal table.
  4. For ALV, use SALV. See YSAMPLE_OO_SALV for sample syntax.

 

Report Fields

Column

Description

Source Table

Field

Comments

Sort Criteria

1

Material

MARA

MATNR

Hotspot to MM03

1 ASC

2

Mat Description

MAKT

MAKTX

Log on Language; Display Material Description when checkbox is marked.

 

3

Plant

MARC &

T001W

WERKS &

NAME1

Concatenate Plant and Name to display ‘Plant – Name’. Name is based on Log on Language

2 ASC

4

Created On

MARA

ERSDA

MM/DD/YYYY format

3 DESC

5

Created By

MARA

ERNAM

 

 

6

Changed On

MARA

LAEDA

MM/DD/YYYY format

4 DESC

7

Changed By

MARA

AENAM

 

 

8

Maint. status

MARC

PSTAT

 

 

9

MRP Type

MARC

DISMM

 

 

10

MRP Controller

MARC

DISPO

 

 

11

Lot size

MARC

DISLS

 

 

12

Procurement

MARC

BESKZ

 

 

13

Ctry of origin

MARC

HERKL

 

 

14

Cstg Lot Size

MARC

LOSGR

 

 

15

Period Ind.

MARC

PERKZ

 

 

16

Material Type

MARA

MTART

 

 

17

Industry Sector

MARA

MBRSH

 

 

18

Material Group

MARA

MATKL

 

 

19

Old matl number

MARA

BISMT

 

 

20

Base Unit

MARA

MEINS

 

 

 

Basic SALV Syntax:

             cl_salv_table=>factory(

                          IMPORTING

                                       r_salv_table = DATA(lo_alv)

                          CHANGING

                                       t_table      = lt_final_output ).

 

lo_alv->display( ). “Display the ALV Grid

 

Note: TRY. CATCH. ENDTRY(open the link to read further)  is important to prevent possible runtime errors.          

8.     Go back to START-OF-SELECTION, create an object or instance of the view class. Call the method for WRITE when radio button ‘Display report with WRITE’ is selected else Call the method for SALV when radio button ‘Display report as ALV’ is selected.

9.     In the same method to display report ALV, add ALV functionality below. See YSAMPLE_OO_SALV for sample syntax:

  1. Set main heading: Material Master Report
  2. Add subheading below:

Material Plant View

Executed by: <sy-uname>

Executed on: <sy-datum> - <sy-uzeit>

Note: Date is in MM/DD/YYYY format. Time is in usual SY-UZEIT (military time) format (For ex: 04/28/2023 – 14:00:01).

Be reminded that Subheading loads slower in Eclipse, not because of performance issue. Subheading loads normal when run in SAP GUI.

 

  1. Add hotspot on Material column. Set the parameter ID value first like below:

SET PARAMETER ID 'MAT' FIELD lv_field.

Then use syntax, CALL TRANSACTION to ‘MM03’ WITH AUTHORITY-CHECK and SKIP FIRST SCREEN. When Material is clicked, screen MM03 is displayed with the Material selected.

  1. Enable default ALV toolbar functions.
  2. Set YELLOW colour for Material column. 
  3. Optimize column width.
  4. Set zebra stripes layout.
  5. Add ALV sorting. Follow sorting criteria column in ‘Report Fields’ below. ASC – Ascending order while DESC – Descending order.
  6. Display Material Description when checkbox is marked.
  7. Field/Column descriptions should be in text elements and not hardcoded.
  8. Don’t display ALV output if there are no data present. Display error ‘No data found’ in the status bar.
  9. Do component testing and make sure logic is working well.

 

*******************************************************************************************************

 

 

Sample Output (There might be differences with the actual requirement)

entry.title: S/4 HANA Application job scheduling based on condition/event
entry.content:
Hi,

S/4 HANA private cloud system.

We are checking the option to schedule the S/4 HANA application job based on some condition/event because we are having some dependency from other systems job completion status (SuccessFactor, IBP, etc.,) .

We didn't see the event/condition based scheduling options in Application Jobs FIORI app.

Is there any possibility to start S/4 HANA application jobs based on completion of dependency jobs from other systems?

Best Regards,

Anand

entry.title: Re: Bulk PDF Generation [thousands of unique customer sta...
entry.content: Could you share with us the approximate cost for licensing InfoBurst?

entry.title: Re: Excel template in BTP BAS tool giving error when depl...
entry.content:
You need to add the file .Ui5RepositoryTextFiles in the webapp folder and add the extension you would like to add

 

^.*.csv$
^.*.jpeg$

 

Example:

https://github.com/spreadsheetimporter/packed-deployment-abap/blob/main/webapp/.Ui5RepositoryTextFiles

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for MacOS
entry.content:
Hi @Stevica 

Perhaps it's worth looking at  dylan-drummond's blog 
Notes on installing SAPGUI for Java for macOS 

From the screenshot it looks like their may be an SNC related library missing but I'm sure Dylan's blog will be more helpful than that.

Gavin

entry.title: Re: Excel template in BTP BAS tool giving error when depl...
entry.content: That is justified criticism, but not an answer to the question. Please comment this under the question

entry.title: Re: New type of CDS entity available: CDS projection views
entry.content: This article is really amazing, it solved a lot of confusion around Projection view. Thanks @AndreaUS

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: @DeoKumar What badges do you think you are missing? I can't report issues unless I know what to report.

entry.title: Packing specifications SAP EWM
entry.content:
I have a requirement that packing required for a product1 that received from Vendor1 and no packing for the same product1 from different vendor2 before final putaway.

what settings that required to pack and no pack for the same product from two different vendors?

entry.title: Error: update-rc.d: error: unkown option
entry.content:
An error occurred during the installation of the SAP HANA Server:

Error checking sapinit
   Starting external program update-rc.d
      Command line is: update-rc.d -n sapinit defaults
      Output line 1: update-rc.d: error: unkown option
      Output line 2: usage: update-rc.d [-f] <basename> remove
      Output line 3:        update-rc.d [-f] <basename> defaults
      Output line 4:        update-rc.d [-f] <basename> defaults-disabled
      Output line 5:        update-rc.d <basename> disable|enable [S|2|3|4|5]
      Output line 6:            -f: force
      Output line 7:
      Output line 8: The disable|enable API is not stable and might change in the future.
      Program terminted with exit code 1

The server is running on Debian 12. I have already tried the workaround without success.

Are there any ideas or solutions to solve the problem?

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: @thomas_jung  Facing this technical error in Create a Business Process

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: Another technical error in this Create and Configure Forms

entry.title: Re: sap fiori road map on on-premise and cloud platform
entry.content: Please provide more information about what exactly you want to achieve and what your current status is. There is still some information missing to help you.

entry.title: Re: TM 9.4, VSR_1STEP doesn't consider single resource assignment to carrier
entry.content: Hi, Have you found the solution where VSR consider owner of the resource? Where owner is a carrier.

entry.title: Re: Files in the change folder are not loaded when I deploy
entry.content: Those files are coming from lrep. In the flpsandbox.js it is maybe mocked. Are you sure that lrep is connected on your FLP? Is there a error message in the console that may relate to that?

entry.title: Re: how to add column in mdc table in sapui5 visual editor?
entry.content: Depends on the app, but it maybe easier to do all that on the backend with CDS extension?

entry.title: Re: Realtime (delta) Data Replication from SAP Analytics Cloud to BW/4 HANA
entry.content:
@andreas_tidl2 

it is not clear 100 mio records from SAC to BW is the initial loading or delta. I assume it is initial loading and that is not a very high value. I suggest you start with DP Agent memory 12 GB and monitor the hardware usage closely afterwards.

 

 

regards

Zili

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: Create a process condition

entry.title: Order address is not getting updated in PR for service operation
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

We have done config for service operation by having order as priority 1 but the order address is not getting updated in PR. Can you please help us on this. 

Regards, 

Preethi

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities
entry.content: Run the business process
